description: The North Side Gang was a multi-ethnic criminal organization in Chicago during Prohibition, active from the early 1920s to the mid-1930s. It was the principal rival of the Chicago Outfit led by Al Capone. Founded by Dean O'Banion and Hymie Weiss, the gang controlled bootlegging, gambling, and robbery on Chicago's North Side. The gang was largely defunct by 1935 after a series of violent conflicts.
